reading: The DECISIVE tile-cost measurement (adversarial review of the 7a/7b findings). A COMPACT order-N full matmul-identity decide is the block-identity building block the Schur-tiling path needs. Order-60 with ~1000-bit entries: DID NOT verify within the 160s cap. Entry-size penalty at order-60 (1000-bit / 8-bit): n/a — so entry size is NOT free; large entries materially slow the compact decide. NET: realistic full-rank, large-entry order-60 tiles are HEAVIER than the borrowed r=8/8-bit point -> the effective tiling ceiling drops below 60 for real tiles; the tiling de-risk is downgraded (k rises, budget grows). Confirms the adversarial review's HIGH-severity gap.
